title: 10 best herbal teas for hyperacidity
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hyperacidity are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the symptoms of hyperacidity. Hyperacidity occurs when there is an excess production of stomach acid, leading to discomfort, pain, and other digestive issues. Herbal teas like chamomile, ginger, licorice, and marshmallow root are some of the popular choices for treating hyperacidity. These teas work by reducing inflammation, coating the stomach lining, and promoting digestion. By drinking these teas, individuals can experience relief from symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. As a result, they can enjoy improved digestion, reduced discomfort, and an overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. It also stimulates the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and prevent the buildup of stomach acid. Additionally, yarrow tea has been shown to have astringent properties, which can help to tighten and tone the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, providing further protection against hyperacidity. Furthermore, yarrow tea contains flavonoids, which have been found to have gastroprotective effects, helping to prevent and heal stomach ulcers. Drinking yarrow tea regularly can help to alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining caused by hyperacidity.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, from tannins, help constrict and tighten tissues, protecting the stomach lining and reducing acid production.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow tea's antispasmodic properties, because of volatile oils like chamazulene, can help relax gastrointestinal muscles, preventing spasms and reducing acid reflux.
Carminative: The carminative properties of yarrow tea, from essential oils like azulene, can help expel gas from the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
Bitter Stimulant: Yarrow tea's bitter taste, from compounds like lactones, promotes the production of digestive enzymes and bile, improving overall digestion and reducing acidity.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow herb, adjusting the amount based on the desired tea strength.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the yarrow herb in a teapot or cup.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds from the yarrow.
Strain the tea into a cup to remove the spent herb, leaving only the liquid.
Drink the yarrow tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for managing hyperacidity symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce acid production in the stomach.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that protect the stomach lining and promote digestive health.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with calming properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce acidity.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining.
Rhizomes: Rich in volatile oils and alkaloids that can help regulate stomach acid production and promote digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains anti-inflammatory and anti-secretory properties that aid in reducing excessive stomach acid production. It also stimulates the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, while preventing the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. Furthermore, mugwort tea has calming effects on the gastrointestinal system, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort. Drinking mugwort tea regularly can provide relief from hyperacidity and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort tea helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is attributed to compounds like chamazulene and flavonoids present in mugwort.
Antacid: Mugwort tea has the ability to neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from hyperacidity. This is due to the presence of calcium and other minerals in mugwort.

Mucilaginous: Mugwort tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the stomach lining. This property helps prevent stomach irritation and is particularly beneficial for individuals with hyperacidity.

Carminative: Mugwort tea helps expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with hyperacidity. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as camphor and thujone in mugwort.

Cholagogue: Mugwort tea stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and the breakdown of fats. This property is important for hyperacidity as it can help improve overall digestive function and prevent the buildup of stomach acid.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried mugwort herb to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the mugwort herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer, discarding the used herb.
Drink the mugwort tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for hyperacidity relief.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Mugwort rhizomes are commonly used in medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Leaves: The leaves of mugwort contain active compounds that help reduce gastric acid production, making them suitable for hyperacidity relief.
Flowers: Mugwort flowers are used in teas for hyperacidity because they have calming effects on the stomach lining and promote healthy digestion.
Seeds: Mugwort seeds are rich in essential oils and have been shown to improve digestion and reduce acidity in the stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. It also has a natural cooling effect on the body, which can help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. Additionally, white tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of acid-producing bacteria in the gut, further helping to prevent hyperacidity. Its mild flavor and lack of bitterness make it a gentle and delicious option for those looking for a natural remedy to manage their symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent further irritation and inflammation. The antioxidants in white tea include catechins and epicatechins.

Antacid: White tea has natural antacid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help relieve symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion. The antacid properties of white tea are due to its high mineral content, including calcium, magnesium, and potassium.

Astringent: White tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help reduce acid production and secretion. The astringent properties of white tea are due to its tannin content.

Gastric protective: White tea has gastric protective properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by hyperacidity, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent further complications such as ulcers and gastritis. The gastric protective properties of white tea are due to the presence of catechins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried herbal white tea leaves or 1 tea bag.
Steep - Let the tea steep for 3-5 minutes to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to be released.
Strain - Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used tea leaves.
Add - Optionally, add a slice of lemon or a teaspoon of honey for taste.
Consume - Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results in managing hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: White tea leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to help balance stomach acidity levels, making them ideal for medicinal teas to treat hyperacidity.
Buds: The unopened buds of the white tea plant contain high concentrations of flavonoids and polyphenols, which can help reduce inflammation and regulate stomach acid production.
Rhyzomes: White tea rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including hyperacidity, due to their anti-inflammatory and acid-regulating properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains several active compounds, including inulin, polyphenols, and terpenoids, that can help regulate gastric acid secretion and promote digestion. Inulin, a type of dietary fiber found in chicory root, can increase the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which may improve overall gut health and help reduce symptoms of acid reflux. Polyphenols and terpenoids, on the other hand, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the lining of the stomach and reduce inflammation. Moreover, chicory tea has a soothing effect on the digestive system and can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain. Overall, chicory tea can be a natural and effective remedy for managing hyperacidity and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of phenolic compounds, can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining caused by hyperacidity.
Mucilage content: The mucilage content in chicory tea coats the stomach lining, protecting it from the harmful effects of hyperacidity.
Cholagogue action: Chicory tea stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and helps prevent hyperacidity.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in chicory tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress caused by hyperacidity.
Bitterness: The bitter compounds in chicory tea, such as sesquiterpene lactones, stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help prevent hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried chicory root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the chicory root particles.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to cool before swallowing, for best results drink 2-3 times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots are commonly used in medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Chicory leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been known to help reduce stomach acidity, making them an ideal ingredient for acid reflux teas.
Flowers: Chicory flowers contain various beneficial compounds, including flavonoids, that can help balance stomach acidity levels and promote digestive health.
Seeds: Chicory seeds are a great source of fiber and essential oils, which can help regulate stomach acid production and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains natural herbs that have antacid properties, which can neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of acid reflux. The tea's ingredients, such as licorice root, marshmallow root, and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe the digestive tract and provide relief from discomfort. Additionally, Eureka tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing hyperacidity. Its natural ingredients work together to promote healthy digestion and alleviate the symptoms of hyperacidity, providing a natural and effective solution for those looking for relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eureka tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can be irritated by hyperacidity, thus providing relief from symptoms. This is largely due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins in the tea.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Eureka tea help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate hyperacidity. The high levels of catechins and other polyphenols in the tea contribute to these antioxidant effects.

Astringent: Eureka tea has astringent properties that help decrease excessive secretions in the stomach, including gastric acid, thereby reducing hyperacidity. This is mainly due to the tannins present in the tea.

Gastric Protective: The gastric protective properties of Eureka tea help strengthen the mucosal lining of the stomach, providing a barrier against harmful effects of gastric acid. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins in the tea.

Digestive: Eureka tea aids in digestion by promoting the production of digestive enzymes, which can help neutralize excessive gastric acid and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. This is largely due to the presence of volatile oils and bitter compounds in the tea.
remedy_recipe: Chop one inch of ginger root and two fresh aloe vera leaves, then combine with four cups of water in a pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and one cinnamon stick to the pot, then let it steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey or lemon juice to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed for hyperacidity relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eureka leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to their high tannin content, which can help reduce stomach acid.
Stems: The stems of the eureka plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe irritated stomach lining, making them useful for treating hyperacidity.
Flowers: Eureka flowers are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive stomach acid.
Seeds: The seeds of the eureka plant are a good source of mucilage, which can help coat and protect the stomach lining from hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Key lime tea helps with hyperacidity because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and hyperacidity. The citric acid present in key limes can help stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and preventing the buildup of stomach acid. Additionally, key lime tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing hyperacidity. The herbal components of the tea may also help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and providing relief from the discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Key lime tea can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort caused by excess stomach acid. This property is attributed to the limonoids and flavonoids present in key limes.

Antioxidant: Key lime tea has high antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, thus reducing the risk of hyperacidity. The antioxidants in key limes include ascorbic acid, flavonoids, and limonoids.

Astringent: Key lime tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the stomach lining, reducing its sensitivity to acid. This property is important for hyperacidity as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. The astringent properties of key limes are due to the tannins they contain.

Carminative: Key lime tea can help reduce gas and bloating in the stomach, which is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent the buildup of stomach acid. The carminative properties of key limes are attributed to the limonene and citral present in the fruit.

Digestive: Key lime tea can stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can help break down food and prevent the buildup of stomach acid. This property is important for hyperacidity as it can help prevent symptoms such as indigestion and bloating. The digestive properties of key limes are due to the citric acid and malic acid present in the fruit.
remedy_recipe: Infuse one teaspoon of dried and crushed calendula flowers in boiling water for 5 minutes to soothe the stomach lining.
Steep two teaspoons of dried and chopped lemon balm leaves in the same water for 3-5 minutes to reduce acid production.
Add two slices of key lime, including the peel, to the mixture and let it steep for another minute for added flavor and vitamin C.
Strain the tea, then add one teaspoon of raw honey to sweeten and further coat the stomach.
Consume the herbal key lime tea while still warm, up to three times a day as needed for hyperacidity relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in citric acid and flavonoids, the leaves help balance stomach acidity.
Rhyzomes: Contain high amounts of starch and have anti-inflammatory properties, soothing the digestive tract.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, the flowers aid in maintaining a healthy digestive system.
Seeds: Contain essential oils, fiber, and magnesium, helping to reduce acid reflux symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and improve digestion. Additionally, turmeric tea can help stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which is necessary for the digestion of fats and can help prevent the buildup of acid in the stomach. Furthermore, turmeric tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess stomach acid. Drinking turmeric tea regularly can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the active ingredient in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate hyperacidity.
Mucosa protective: Turmeric extract has been shown to protect the gastric mucosa, reducing the risk of damage from stomach acid.
Antioxidant: Turmeric possesses strong antioxidant properties, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, often produced in response to hyperacidity.
Antisecretory: Turmeric can help reduce gastric acid secretion, which is beneficial for individuals with hyperacidity.
Gastroprotective: Turmeric has been found to have gastroprotective effects, helping to prevent and treat gastric ulcers, a common complication of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of grated fresh turmeric root and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds and 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root, simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem or rhizome is the most commonly used part for making medicinal teas for hyperacidity due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Turmeric roots, although similar to rhizomes, have slightly different medicinal properties and are used in teas to aid digestion and soothe stomach discomfort.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves contain curcumin and are used in teas to help reduce inflammation and improve digestion in cases of hyperacidity.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers are used less frequently, but they do contain curcumin and are sometimes included in teas to help with digestion and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with hyperacidity because it contains glycyrrhizic acid, which can increase the mucus lining of the stomach, protecting it from acid. This can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, providing relief from symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, bloating, and stomach pain. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to help balance stomach acid levels, reducing the production of excess acid that can contribute to hyperacidity. Furthermore, it has soothing properties that can help coat the stomach and esophagus, providing additional relief from acid reflux. However, it is important to note that excessive consumption of licorice tea can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium loss, so it is recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using licorice tea as a treatment for hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Licorice tea forms a protective film on the stomach lining, preventing irritation and reducing hyperacidity. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other polysaccharides.

Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea helps reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to hyperacidity. This is attributed to the compound liquiritigenin and its anti-inflammatory effects.
Mucosal protective: Licorice tea protects the stomach lining from erosion and damage caused by hyperacidity. The flavonoids and volatile oils in licorice are responsible for this property.

Antispasmodic: Licorice tea helps alleviate muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can exacerbate hyperacidity. This is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, which has antispasmodic properties.

Gastric mucosal regeneration: Licorice tea promotes the regeneration of the stomach lining, aiding in the healing process for individuals with hyperacidity. This is attributed to the compound glabridin, which supports gastric mucosal regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root and place it in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the licorice root.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent licorice root.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day between meals to soothe hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which can help balance stomach acid levels and soothe gastrointestinal inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain flavonoids and coumarins that can help protect the stomach lining and reduce acid production.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizic acid, which has been shown to have anti-ulcer effects and can help heal stomach ulcers caused by hyperacidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tea, also known as hops tea, helps with hyperacidity because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Additionally, hops have been shown to increase the production of digestive juices, which can help break down food and prevent the buildup of stomach acid. The tea is also known to have a calming effect on the stomach, which can help reduce symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. Furthermore, hops contain bitter acids that can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving overall digestion and reducing the likelihood of hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Humulus lupulus, also known as hops, contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as alpha and beta acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from hyperacidity.

Antispasmodic: The herb has antispasmodic properties due to the presence of essential oils and bitter acids, which can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity caused by spasms.

Astringent: Humulus lupulus contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help protect the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with hyperacidity.

Anxiolytic: The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and volatile oils that exhibit anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, thus minimizing the risk of developing hyperacidity caused by increased stress hormones.

Analgesic: Humulus lupulus has analgesic properties due to the presence of essential oils and bitter acids, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hyperacidity, providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried hops (Humulus lupulus) flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the hops solids.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day between meals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Humulus lupulus flowers contain bitter acids that can help reduce stomach acid and aid in digestion.
Leaves: The leaves of the hop plant have been used in teas to treat hyperacidity due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Humulus lupulus seeds are rich in oils and compounds that can help soothe gastrointestinal inflammation and provide relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the hop plant contain compounds that can help regulate stomach acid production, making them useful in medicinal teas for hyperacidity.
Bark: The bark of the hop plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, including hyperacidity, due to its calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperacidity with herbal teas is by consuming chamomile, marshmallow root, and licorice root teas. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. Additionally, they promote the production of mucus in the stomach, which can protect the stomach lining from acid. Drinking these teas after meals and before bedtime can help prevent acid reflux and provide relief from hyperacidity. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux, gastritis, and indigestion. Herbal teas such as chamomile, ginger, and licorice root are known for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and esophagus, and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. Peppermint tea can also aid in digestion and alleviate gas, but should be avoided if experiencing acid reflux as it can worsen symptoms. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
